summary: FPV drones from Ukraine's 36th Marine Brigade survived a Russian attack near Kostiantinivka, with operators using drone detectors to anticipate the strike. Russian forces used FPV drones to kill two soldiers on motorcycles assaulting Ukrainian positions and later eliminated a Russian soldier who feigned surrender before fleeing. The Rubicon Center reported 129 enemy targets destroyed on April 18, 2026, including personnel and fixed-wing UAVs across Donbas, Krasny Liman, Belgorod, and Sumy directions, while also conducting remote mining and coordinating with Russian Aerospace Forces. Ukrainian forces captured a Ukrainian Armed Forces Shark-M fixed-wing UAV after its parachute deployed during a kinetic interception by the Russian "Yolka" air defense drone in the Krasny Liman area. Discussions highlighted the use of aerial relay drones to extend FPV strike ranges and the need for anti-radiation missile-equipped drones to counter dense radar networks.
